title: LEGAL SERVICES - 211info
text: OREGON CRIME VICTIMS LAW CENTER LEGAL SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides victims of crime with legal help in asserting and enforcing their rights; assistance with social support services, including those that victims may need to effectively participate in the case against their offender and to help with recovery. Offers advocacy for victims of domestic violence in helping to get a protection order. In limited circumstances, can provide survivors of domestic violence, sexual assault, and stalking with representation in civil protective order cases. Does not provide representation in domestic relation cases, including custody and dissolution. Does not assist with eviction assistance or landlord/tenant issues. Eligibility: Individuals who are victims of a crime who are current residents of Oregon or victims of a crime that was committed in the state of Oregon. The crime must have been filed by the court. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm by appointment only Email (503) 208-8160 Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 208-8160 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for intake or email for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Varies on a case-by-case basis LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: State of Oregon TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: TriMet bus lines 54 and 55 Services: Domestic/Family Violence Legal Services General Crime Victim Assistance General Legal Aid If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. No Mapping Information Available
